message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Selected Row Style Fix

## Context
The selected checkpoint row currently has a thick left border "tab" indicator. User wants to remove that border and use a background color instead.

## Changes — `pkg/ui/dashboard/styles.go`

**`selectedRowStyle`** (line 88): Remove `BorderLeft`, `BorderStyle`, `BorderForeground`. Add `Background(lipgloss.Color("236"))` (dark gray). Set `PaddingLeft(3)` to match `normalRowStyle`.

## Verification
- `make build`
- Visual: selected row has subtle dark background, no left border


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/arvos/.claude/projects/-Users-arvos-Tactic-workspaces-workspace-entire-local-cli-entire-local/5c50dc73-4b5e-4aa6-b0fa-e5348efd9356.jsonl

planContent:
# Plan: Selected Row Style Fix

## Context
The selected checkpoint row currently has a thick left border "tab" indicator. User wants to remove that border and use a background color instead.

## Changes — `pkg/ui/dashboard/styles.go`

**`selectedRowStyle`** (line 88): Remove `BorderLeft`, `BorderStyle`, `BorderForeground`. Add `Background(lipgloss.Color("236"))` (dark gray). Set `PaddingLeft(3)` to match `normalRowStyle`.

## Verification
- `make build`
- Visual: selected row has subtle dark background, no left border

file.content:
package dashboard

import "github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"

var (
	primary = lipgloss.Color("208")
	accent  = lipgloss.Color("214")
	success = lipgloss.Color("46")
	errClr  = lipgloss.Color("196")
	info    = lipgloss.Color("75")
	subtle  = lipgloss.Color("241")

	headerStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(primary).
			Bold(true).
			Padding(0, 1)

	footerStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(subtle).
			Padding(0, 1)

	panelStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
			BorderForeground(subtle).
			Padding(0, 1)

	statCardStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
			BorderForeground(subtle).
			Padding(0, 1)

	statValueStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(accent).
			Bold(true)

	statLabelStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(subtle)

	agentBadgeStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(primary).
			Bold(true)

	branchStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(info).
			Bold(true)

	hashStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(subtle)

	addStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(success)

	delStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(errClr)

	toolBadgeStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(subtle).
			Italic(true)

	helpKeyStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(accent).
			Bold(true)

	helpDescStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(subtle)

	filterStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(primary).
			Bold(true)

	dimStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(subtle)

	tabActiveStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(primary).
			Bold(true)

	tabInactiveStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
				Foreground(subtle)

	sectionHeaderStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
				Foreground(accent).
				Bold(true)

	scrollIndicatorStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
				Foreground(subtle)

	selectedRowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
				BorderLeft(true).
				BorderStyle(lipgloss.ThickBorder()).
				BorderForeground(primary).
				PaddingLeft(1)

	normalRowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			PaddingLeft(3)
)

input.old_string:
selectedRowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
				BorderLeft(true).
				BorderStyle(lipgloss.ThickBorder()).
				BorderForeground(primary).
				PaddingLeft(1)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
package dashboard

import "github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"

var (
	primary = lipgloss.Color("208")
	accent  = lipgloss.Color("214")
	success = lipgloss.Color("46")
	errClr  = lipgloss.Color("196")
	info    = lipgloss.Color("75")
	subtle  = lipgloss.Color("241")

	headerStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(primary).
			Bold(true).
			Padding(0, 1)

	footerStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(subtle).
			Padding(0, 1)

	panelStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
			BorderForeground(subtle).
			Padding(0, 1)

	statCardStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
			BorderForeground(subtle).
			Padding(0, 1)

	statValueStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(accent).
			Bold(true)

	statLabelStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(subtle)

	agentBadgeStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(primary).
			Bold(true)

	branchStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(info).
			Bold(true)

	hashStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(subtle)

	addStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(success)

	delStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(errClr)

	toolBadgeStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(subtle).
			Italic(true)

	helpKeyStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(accent).
			Bold(true)

	helpDescStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(subtle)

	filterStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(primary).
			Bold(true)

	dimStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(subtle)

	tabActiveStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			Foreground(primary).
			Bold(true)

	tabInactiveStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
				Foreground(subtle)

	sectionHeaderStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
				Foreground(accent).
				Bold(true)

	scrollIndicatorStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
				Foreground(subtle)

	selectedRowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
				BorderLeft(true).
				BorderStyle(lipgloss.ThickBorder()).
				BorderForeground(primary).
				PaddingLeft(1)

	normalRowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
			PaddingLeft(3)
)

content.text: Build succeeds. The `selectedRowStyle` now uses a dark gray background (`#236`) with `PaddingLeft(3)` matching `normalRowStyle`, instead of the thick left border.

- The background color is not on the entire row
- Could we add a border on the selection?
 - I'm unsure if I want the border to move the other rows when navigating
Let me explore how rows are rendered to understand the width issue and how borders would work.

content.content:
1→package dashboard
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"strings"
     6→
     7→	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/key"
     8→	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
     9→	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/jcleira/entire-local/pkg/checkpoint"
    12→)
    13→
    14→type model struct {
    15→	loader     *checkpoint.Loader
    16→	summaries  []checkpoint.CheckpointSummary
    17→	stats      checkpoint.OverviewStats
    18→	detail     *checkpoint.Checkpoint
    19→	transcript []checkpoint.TranscriptEntry
    20→
    21→	screen       screen
    22→	cursor       int
    23→	width        int
    24→	height       int
    25→	filter       string
    26→	filtering    bool
    27→	showHelp     bool
    28→	err          error
    29→	detailTab    detailTab
    30→	scrollOffset int
    31→	maxScroll    int
    32→}
    33→
    34→func RunDashboard(loader *checkpoint.Loader) error {
    35→	m := model{
    36→		loader: loader,
    37→		screen: screenOverview,
    38→		width:  80,
    39→		height: 24,
    40→	}
    41→
    42→	p := tea.NewProgram(m, tea.WithAltScreen())
    43→	_, err := p.Run()
    44→	return err
    45→}
    46→
    47→func (m model) Init() tea.Cmd {
    48→	return m.loadCheckpoints()
    49→}
    50→
    51→func (m model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
    52→	switch msg := msg.(type) {
    53→	case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
    54→		m.width = msg.Width
    55→		m.height = msg.Height
    56→		return m, nil
    57→
    58→	case tea.KeyMsg:
    59→		if m.filtering {
    60→			return m.handleFilterInput(msg)
    61→		}
    62→		return m.handleKeyPress(msg)
    63→
    64→	case checkpointsLoadedMsg:
    65→		m.summaries = msg.summaries
    66→		m.stats = checkpoint.ComputeStats(msg.summaries)
    67→		return m, nil
    68→
    69→	case checkpointDetailLoadedMsg:
    70→		m.detail = msg.checkpoint
    71→		return m, nil
    72→
    73→	case transcriptLoadedMsg:
    74→		m.transcript = msg.entries
    75→		return m, nil
    76→
    77→	case errMsg:
    78→		m.err = msg.err
    79→		return m, nil
    80→	}
    81→
    82→	return m, nil
    83→}
    84→
    85→func (m model) View() string {
    86→	if m.showHelp {
    87→		return renderHelp(m.width, m.height)
    88→	}
    89→
    90→	if m.err != nil {
    91→		return lipgloss.Place(m.width, m.height,
    92→			lipgloss.Center, lipgloss.Center,
    93→			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(errClr).Render("Error: "+m.err.Error()),
    94→		)
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	switch m.screen {
    98→	case screenOverview:
    99→		return m.renderOverviewScreen()
   100→	case screenDetail:
   101→		content := renderCheckpointDetail(m.detail, m.transcript, m.width, m.height-2, m.detailTab, m.scrollOffset)
   102→		footer := m.renderFooter()
   103→		return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, content, footer)
   104→	}
   105→
   106→	return ""
   107→}
   108→
   109→func (m model) handleKeyPress(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
   110→	switch {
   111→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Quit):
   112→		return m, tea.Quit
   113→
   114→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Help):
   115→		m.showHelp = !m.showHelp
   116→		return m, nil
   117→
   118→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Refresh):
   119→		return m, m.loadCheckpoints()
   120→
   121→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Filter):
   122→		if m.screen == screenOverview {
   123→			m.filtering = true
   124→			return m, nil
   125→		}
   126→	}
   127→
   128→	switch m.screen {
   129→	case screenOverview:
   130→		return m.handleOverviewKeys(msg)
   131→	case screenDetail:
   132→		return m.handleDetailKeys(msg)
   133→	}
   134→
   135→	return m, nil
   136→}
   137→
   138→func (m model) handleOverviewKeys(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
   139→	filtered := filterSummaries(m.summaries, m.filter)
   140→
   141→	switch {
   142→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Down):
   143→		if m.cursor < len(filtered)-1 {
   144→			m.cursor++
   145→		}
   146→
   147→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Up):
   148→		if m.cursor > 0 {
   149→			m.cursor--
   150→		}
   151→
   152→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Enter):
   153→		if len(filtered) > 0 && m.cursor < len(filtered) {
   154→			selected := filtered[m.cursor]
   155→			m.screen = screenDetail
   156→			m.detail = nil
   157→			m.transcript = nil
   158→			m.detailTab = tabOverview
   159→			m.scrollOffset = 0
   160→			return m, tea.Batch(
   161→				m.loadDetail(selected.ID),
   162→				m.loadTranscript(selected.ID),
   163→			)
   164→		}
   165→	}
   166→
   167→	return m, nil
   168→}
   169→
   170→func (m model) handleDetailKeys(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
   171→	switch {
   172→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Back):
   173→		m.screen = screenOverview
   174→		m.detail = nil
   175→		m.transcript = nil
   176→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   177→		return m, nil
   178→
   179→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Tab):
   180→		m.detailTab = (m.detailTab + 1) % 4
   181→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   182→		return m, nil
   183→
   184→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.ShiftTab):
   185→		m.detailTab = (m.detailTab + 3) % 4
   186→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   187→		return m, nil
   188→
   189→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Num1):
   190→		m.detailTab = tabOverview
   191→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   192→		return m, nil
   193→
   194→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Num2):
   195→		m.detailTab = tabFiles
   196→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   197→		return m, nil
   198→
   199→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Num3):
   200→		m.detailTab = tabTranscript
   201→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   202→		return m, nil
   203→
   204→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Num4):
   205→		m.detailTab = tabPlan
   206→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   207→		return m, nil
   208→
   209→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Down):
   210→		if m.scrollOffset < m.maxScroll {
   211→			m.scrollOffset++
   212→		}
   213→		return m, nil
   214→
   215→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Up):
   216→		if m.scrollOffset > 0 {
   217→			m.scrollOffset--
   218→		}
   219→		return m, nil
   220→	}
   221→
   222→	return m, nil
   223→}
   224→
   225→func (m model) handleFilterInput(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
   226→	switch msg.Type {
   227→	case tea.KeyEnter, tea.KeyEscape:
   228→		m.filtering = false
   229→		if msg.Type == tea.KeyEscape {
   230→			m.filter = ""
   231→		}
   232→		m.cursor = 0
   233→		return m, nil
   234→	case tea.KeyBackspace:
   235→		if m.filter != "" {
   236→			m.filter = m.filter[:len(m.filter)-1]
   237→		}
   238→		m.cursor = 0
   239→		return m, nil
   240→	default:
   241→		if len(msg.Runes) > 0 {
   242→			m.filter += string(msg.Runes)
   243→			m.cursor = 0
   244→		}
   245→		return m, nil
   246→	}
   247→}
   248→
   249→func (m model) renderOverviewScreen() string {
   250→	panelWidth := m.width - 2
   251→
   252→	overview := renderOverview(m.stats, panelWidth-2)
   253→	overviewPanel := panelStyle.Width(panelWidth).Render(overview)
   254→	overviewHeight := lipgloss.Height(overviewPanel)
   255→
   256→	footerContent := m.renderFooterContent()
   257→	footerPanel := panelStyle.Width(panelWidth).Render(footerContent)
   258→	footerHeight := lipgloss.Height(footerPanel)
   259→
   260→	listPanelOuter := m.height - overviewHeight - footerHeight
   261→	if listPanelOuter < 5 {
   262→		listPanelOuter = 5
   263→	}
   264→	listInnerHeight := listPanelOuter - 2
   265→	if listInnerHeight < 3 {
   266→		listInnerHeight = 3
   267→	}
   268→
   269→	contentWidth := panelWidth - 2
   270→	list := renderCheckpointList(m.summaries, m.cursor, listInnerHeight, m.filter, contentWidth)
   271→	listPanel := panelStyle.Width(panelWidth).Height(listPanelOuter - 2).Render(list)
   272→
   273→	return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, overviewPanel, listPanel, footerPanel)
   274→}
   275→
   276→func (m model) renderFooter() string {
   277→	if m.filtering {
   278→		return footerStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("Filter: %s█", m.filter))
   279→	}
   280→
   281→	var keybinds string
   282→	switch m.screen {
   283→	case screenOverview:
   284→		keybinds = "j/k navigate  enter select  / filter  ? help  q quit"
   285→	case screenDetail:
   286→		keybinds = "esc back  j/k scroll  tab switch  1-4 tabs  ? help  q quit"
   287→	}
   288→
   289→	return footerStyle.Render(dimStyle.Render(keybinds))
   290→}
   291→
   292→func (m model) renderFooterContent() string {
   293→	if m.filtering {
   294→		return fmt.Sprintf("Filter: %s█", m.filter)
   295→	}
   296→
   297→	leftSide := fmt.Sprintf("%d checkpoints", m.stats.TotalCheckpoints)
   298→	if m.stats.TotalTokens > 0 {
   299→		leftSide += "  ·  " + formatTokensShort(m.stats.TotalTokens)
   300→	}
   301→
   302→	rightSide := "j/k navigate  enter select  / filter  ? help  q quit"
   303→
   304→	padding := m.width - 6 - lipgloss.Width(leftSide) - lipgloss.Width(rightSide)
   305→	if padding < 2 {
   306→		padding = 2
   307→	}
   308→
   309→	return leftSide + strings.Repeat(" ", padding) + dimStyle.Render(rightSide)
   310→}
   311→
   312→func (m model) loadCheckpoints() tea.Cmd {
   313→	return func() tea.Msg {
   314→		summaries, err := m.loader.ListCheckpoints()
   315→		if err != nil {
   316→			return errMsg{err}
   317→		}
   318→		return checkpointsLoadedMsg{summaries}
   319→	}
   320→}
   321→
   322→func (m model) loadDetail(id string) tea.Cmd {
   323→	return func() tea.Msg {
   324→		cp, err := m.loader.LoadCheckpoint(id)
   325→		if err != nil {
   326→			return errMsg{err}
   327→		}
   328→		return checkpointDetailLoadedMsg{cp}
   329→	}
   330→}
   331→
   332→func (m model) loadTranscript(id string) tea.Cmd {
   333→	return func() tea.Msg {
   334→		entries, err := m.loader.LoadTranscript(id)
   335→		if err != nil {
   336→			return errMsg{err}
   337→		}
   338→		return transcriptLoadedMsg{entries}
   339→	}
   340→}
   341→

content.text:
Perfect! I now have all the files needed. Let me provide you with a comprehensive analysis of how checkpoint rows are rendered.

## Summary of Findings

### 1. Row Styling in `checkpoint_list.go` (lines 50-60)

The `renderCheckpointList` function applies styles to **the full multi-line block** returned by `renderCheckpointBlock`:

```go
for i := start; i < end; i++ {
    s := filtered[i]
    block := renderCheckpointBlock(s, contentWidth)

    if i == cursor {
        sb.WriteString(selectedRowStyle.Render(block))
    } else {
        sb.WriteString(normalRowStyle.Render(block))
    }
    sb.WriteString("\n")
}
```

**Key observation**: The style is applied to `block` (the entire rendered checkpoint block), which is a multi-line string (2-3 lines depending on whether there's a subtitle). The style is NOT applied to individual lines—it's applied to the complete block as a single unit.

### 2. Checkpoint Block Structure (lines 69-161)

`renderCheckpointBlock` returns a string containing:
- **Line 1**: Title (bold, displayed via `line1`)
- **Line 2** (optional): Subtitle (if present)
- **Line 3**: Metadata (hash, time, agent, stats)

These are joined with newlines:
```go
if subtitle != "" {
    return line1 + "\n" + subtitle + "\n" + metaLine
}
return line1 + "\n" + metaLine
```

The `metaLine` is calculated with proper spacing (lines 148-155):
```go
metaLine := leftSide
if rightSide != "" {
    padding := width - lipgloss.Width(leftSide) - lipgloss.Width(rightSide)
    if padding < 2 {
        padding = 2
    }
    metaLine = leftSide + strings.Repeat(" ", padding) + rightSide
}
```

### 3. Terminal Width Tracking in `dashboard.go`

The model struct has these fields (lines 14-32):
```go
type model struct {
    ...
    width        int    // Updated by tea.WindowSizeMsg (line 54)
    height       int
    ...
}
```

The `width` is set in the `Update` handler when a `tea.WindowSizeMsg` is received, and is passed down to `renderCheckpointList` (line 270):
```go
contentWidth := panelWidth - 2
list := renderCheckpointList(m.summaries, m.cursor, listInnerHeight, m.filter, contentWidth)
```

The width calculation chain is:
- Model receives actual terminal width in `m.width`
- `renderOverviewScreen()` computes `panelWidth := m.width - 2` (for border padding)
- Passes `contentWidth := panelWidth - 2` to `renderCheckpointList`

### 4. Style Definitions in `styles.go` (lines 88-94)

```go
selectedRowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Background(lipgloss.Color("236")).  // Dark gray background
            PaddingLeft(3)

normalRowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
        PaddingLeft(3)
```

**Critical finding**: 
- `selectedRowStyle` only has `Background()` and `PaddingLeft(3)` — **NO width constraint**
- `normalRowStyle` only has `PaddingLeft(3)` — **NO background, NO width constraint**

### 5. Why Background Doesn't Fill the Terminal Width

The issue is that **Lipgloss styles only apply to the text content they wrap**. The `selectedRowStyle` is applied via `selectedRowStyle.Render(block)`, which:

1. Takes the multi-line `block` string
2. Applies the background color to the visible text content
3. Applies `PaddingLeft(3)` to add 3 spaces on the left
4. **Does NOT pad the lines to fill the terminal width**

Each line in the block (title, subtitle, metaLine) has its own length determined by its content. When the background style is applied:
- Line 1 (title): Gets background only as wide as the title + left padding
- Line 2 (subtitle): Gets background only as wide as the subtitle + left padding
- Line 3 (metaLine): Gets background only as wide as the metaLine + left padding (and metaLine is calculated to fit contentWidth, but lipgloss doesn't auto-pad)

**The metaLine has padding between left and right sides** (line 154), but Lipgloss doesn't know about the terminal width and won't extend the background beyond the actual text length.

To fix this, you would need to use `.Width(width)` on the `selectedRowStyle` to force it to pad/expand to the full width before applying the background.

---

### Complete File Paths
- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-entire-local-cli/entire-local/pkg/ui/dashboard/checkpoint_list.go`
- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-entire-local-cli/entire-local/pkg/ui/dashboard/dashboard.go`
- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-entire-local-cli/entire-local/pkg/ui/dashboard/styles.go`

toolUseResult.prompt:
Explore the checkpoint list rendering in this Go Bubble Tea TUI app. I need to understand:

1. How rows are rendered in `pkg/ui/dashboard/checkpoint_list.go` — specifically how `selectedRowStyle` and `normalRowStyle` are applied to rows, and whether the style is applied to the full row string or just part of it.

2. How the terminal width is available in the model (look at `dashboard.go` for the model struct and how width is tracked).

3. Look at `styles.go` to see the current `selectedRowStyle` and `normalRowStyle` definitions.

I need to understand why a `Background` style might not cover the entire row width — likely the style is only applied to the text content, not padded to fill the terminal width.

Be thorough — read the full checkpoint_list.go and the relevant parts of dashboard.go and styles.go.

## Summary of Findings

### 1. Row Styling in `checkpoint_list.go` (lines 50-60)

The `renderCheckpointList` function applies styles to **the full multi-line block** returned by `renderCheckpointBlock`:

```go
for i := start; i < end; i++ {
    s := filtered[i]
    block := renderCheckpointBlock(s, contentWidth)

    if i == cursor {
        sb.WriteString(selectedRowStyle.Render(block))
    } else {
        sb.WriteString(normalRowStyle.Render(block))
    }
    sb.WriteString("\n")
}
```

### 2. Checkpoint Block Structure (lines 69-161)

`renderCheckpointBlock` returns a string containing:
- **Line 1**: Title (bold, displayed via `line1`)
- **Line 2** (optional): Subtitle (if present)
- **Line 3**: Metadata (hash, time, agent, stats)

These are joined with newlines:
```go
if subtitle != "" {
    return line1 + "\n" + subtitle + "\n" + metaLine
}
return line1 + "\n" + metaLine
```

The `metaLine` is calculated with proper spacing (lines 148-155):
```go
metaLine := leftSide
if rightSide != "" {
    padding := width - lipgloss.Width(leftSide) - lipgloss.Width(rightSide)
    if padding < 2 {
        padding = 2
    }
    metaLine = leftSide + strings.Repeat(" ", padding) + rightSide
}
```

### 3. Terminal Width Tracking in `dashboard.go`

The model struct has these fields (lines 14-32):
```go
type model struct {
    ...
    width        int    // Updated by tea.WindowSizeMsg (line 54)
    height       int
    ...
}
```

The `width` is set in the `Update` handler when a `tea.WindowSizeMsg` is received, and is passed down to `renderCheckpointList` (line 270):
```go

list := renderCheckpointList(m.summaries, m.cursor, listInnerHeight, m.filter, contentWidth)
```

The width calculation chain is:
- Model receives actual terminal width in `m.width`
- `renderOverviewScreen()` computes `panelWidth := m.width - 2` (for border padding)
- Passes `contentWidth := panelWidth - 2` to `renderCheckpointList`

### 4. Style Definitions in `styles.go` (lines 88-94)

```go
selectedRowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Background(lipgloss.Color("236")).  // Dark gray background
            PaddingLeft(3)

normalRowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
        PaddingLeft(3)
```

**Critical finding**: 
- `selectedRowStyle` only has `Background()` and `PaddingLeft(3)` — **NO width constraint**
- `normalRowStyle` only has `PaddingLeft(3)` — **NO background, NO width constraint**

### 5. Why Background Doesn't Fill the Terminal Width

The issue is that **Lipgloss styles only apply to the text content they wrap**. The `selectedRowStyle` is applied via `selectedRowStyle.Render(block)`, which:

1. Takes the multi-line `block` string
2. Applies the background color to the visible text content
3. Applies `PaddingLeft(3)` to add 3 spaces on the left
4. **Does NOT pad the lines to fill the terminal width**

Each line in the block (title, subtitle, metaLine) has its own length determined by its content. When the background style is applied:
- Line 1 (title): Gets background only as wide as the title + left padding
- Line 2 (subtitle): Gets background only as wide as the subtitle + left padding
- Line 3 (metaLine): Gets background only as wide as the metaLine + left padding (and metaLine is calculated to fit contentWidth, but lipgloss doesn't auto-pad)

**The metaLine has padding between left and right sides** (line 154), but Lipgloss doesn't know about the terminal width and won't extend the background beyond the actual text length.

To fix this, you would need to use `.Width(width)` on the `selectedRowStyle` to force it to pad/expand to the full width before applying the background.

---

### Complete File Paths
- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-entire-local-cli/entire-local/pkg/ui/dashboard/checkpoint_list.go`
- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-entire-local-cli/entire-local/pkg/ui/dashboard/dashboard.go`

file.content:
# Plan: Selected Row Style Fix

## Context
The selected checkpoint row currently has a thick left border "tab" indicator. User wants to remove that border and use a background color instead.

## Changes — `pkg/ui/dashboard/styles.go`

**`selectedRowStyle`** (line 88): Remove `BorderLeft`, `BorderStyle`, `BorderForeground`. Add `Background(lipgloss.Color("236"))` (dark gray). Set `PaddingLeft(3)` to match `normalRowStyle`.

## Verification
- `make build`
- Visual: selected row has subtle dark background, no left border

content.text: Now let me read how `renderCheckpointList` calls these styles to confirm the render-time width approach.
Good. I can see `contentWidth` is available at the render site. Now I have everything I need to write the plan.

input.old_string:
# Plan: Selected Row Style Fix

## Context
The selected checkpoint row currently has a thick left border "tab" indicator. User wants to remove that border and use a background color instead.

## Changes — `pkg/ui/dashboard/styles.go`

**`selectedRowStyle`** (line 88): Remove `BorderLeft`, `BorderStyle`, `BorderForeground`. Add `Background(lipgloss.Color("236"))` (dark gray). Set `PaddingLeft(3)` to match `normalRowStyle`.

## Verification
- `make build`
- Visual: selected row has subtle dark background, no left border
input.new_string:
# Plan: Selected Row — Full-Width Background + Left Border

## Context
After removing the thick left border and adding a background color, two issues remain:
1. The background only covers the text content, not the full row width
2. User wants a left border indicator back, but without causing layout shift when navigating

## Root Cause
Lipgloss `Background()` only colors the actual text. To fill the full row, we need `.Width(contentWidth)` applied at render time (since terminal width isn't known at style-definition time).

## Changes

### 1. `pkg/ui/dashboard/styles.go` (line 88)

**`selectedRowStyle`**: Add back `BorderLeft(true)` + `BorderStyle(ThickBorder())` + `BorderForeground(primary)`. Keep `Background(lipgloss.Color("236"))`. Set `PaddingLeft(1)` (border 2 chars + padding 1 = 3, matching normalRowStyle's PaddingLeft(3)).

```go
selectedRowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
    Background(lipgloss.Color("236")).
    BorderLeft(true).
    BorderStyle(lipgloss.ThickBorder()).
    BorderForeground(primary).
    PaddingLeft(1)
```

No change to `normalRowStyle` (already `PaddingLeft(3)`, so total left space is identical — no layout shift).

### 2. `pkg/ui/dashboard/checkpoint_list.go` (line 54-57)

Apply `.Width(contentWidth)` at render time for both styles so the background fills the full row:

```go
if i == cursor {
    sb.WriteString(selectedRowStyle.Width(contentWidth).Render(block))
} else {
    sb.WriteString(normalRowStyle.Width(contentWidth).Render(block))
}
```

## Verification
- `make build`
- Visual: selected row has full-width dark background + orange left border, no layout shift when navigating

input.plan:
# Plan: Selected Row — Full-Width Background + Left Border

## Context
After removing the thick left border and adding a background color, two issues remain:
1. The background only covers the text content, not the full row width
2. User wants a left border indicator back, but without causing layout shift when navigating

## Root Cause
Lipgloss `Background()` only colors the actual text. To fill the full row, we need `.Width(contentWidth)` applied at render time (since terminal width isn't known at style-definition time).

## Changes

### 1. `pkg/ui/dashboard/styles.go` (line 88)

**`selectedRowStyle`**: Add back `BorderLeft(true)` + `BorderStyle(ThickBorder())` + `BorderForeground(primary)`. Keep `Background(lipgloss.Color("236"))`. Set `PaddingLeft(1)` (border 2 chars + padding 1 = 3, matching normalRowStyle's PaddingLeft(3)).

```go
selectedRowStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
    Background(lipgloss.Color("236")).
    BorderLeft(true).
    BorderStyle(lipgloss.ThickBorder()).
    BorderForeground(primary).
    PaddingLeft(1)
```

No change to `normalRowStyle` (already `PaddingLeft(3)`, so total left space is identical — no layout shift).

### 2. `pkg/ui/dashboard/checkpoint_list.go` (line 54-57)

Apply `.Width(contentWidth)` at render time for both styles so the background fills the full row:

```go
if i == cursor {
    sb.WriteString(selectedRowStyle.Width(contentWidth).Render(block))
} else {
    sb.WriteString(normalRowStyle.Width(contentWidth).Render(block))
}
```

## Verification
- `make build`
- Visual: selected row has full-width dark background + orange left border, no layout shift when navigating
